subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Flash Floods
description: Learn Flash Floods in one minute.

hook.text: A flash flood can turn a dry creek bed into a 10-foot wall of water in under an hour.

buildup.text: Heavy rain overwhelms drainage, sending water rushing downhill through narrow channels.

discovery.text: Flash floods are the #1 weather killer in the U.S.—more deadly than tornadoes and hurricanes.

twist.text: Rain can fall miles away and the flood arrives under blue sky—no local warning.

climax.text: Low-lying areas, underpasses, and canyon bottoms become death traps during flash flood events.

punchline.text: Blue sky above. Wall of water coming. Flash floods give no warning.

quiz.question: What makes flash floods particularly dangerous?
options: They can arrive without local rain, under clear skies
They move slowly enough to escape
